creator: mandree@FreeBSD.org
text:
As further data point, I dug a bit deeper into this, to find that
mail/mailman is not at fault here.

There are two factors:

1. mailman's groups are created after package registration - this is not
under mailman's control, but inside the pkgNG rig. (BDrewery figured
this part out).
This appears to be the real cause.

2. files/patch-libpkg__packing.c, as of 1.1.4_8, introduces unchecked
pointer dereferences, which cause NULL pointers to be dereferenced.
This is a symptom that should be fixed for robustness.

Cross-reference: https://github.com/freebsd/pkg/issues/628

After the pkg port was made to respect WITH_DEBUG=yes
<http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=183277>, debugging this was
a snap.  This is from a relevant GDB session, and can be triggered with
"make stage fake-pkg -C /usr/ports/mail/mailman", and hacking the
fake-pkg: target in bsd.pkgng.mk to run gdb --args ${PKG_CMD} instead of
the plain ${PKG_CMD}:

> Program received signal SIGSEGV, Segmentation fault.
> [Switching to Thread 28406300 (LWP 100074/pkg-static)]
> 0x080bac08 in packing_append_file_attr (pack=0x28405150, filepath=0xbfbfdd0f "/usr/ports/mail/mailman/work/stage/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/", newpath=0xbfbfd90e "//usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/", 
>     uname=0x28870801 "root", gname=0x28870813 "mailman", perm=0) at packing.c:196
> warning: Source file is more recent than executable.
> 
> 196				archive_entry_set_gid(entry, gr->gr_gid);

Backtrace:

> (gdb) bt
> #0  0x080bac08 in packing_append_file_attr (pack=0x28405170, filepath=0xbfbfdccf "/usr/ports/mail/mailman/work/stage/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/", newpath=0xbfbfd8ce "//usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/", 
>     uname=0x28870801 "root", gname=0x28870813 "mailman", perm=0) at packing.c:196
> #1  0x080b8b5f in pkg_copy_tree (pkg=0x28431200, src=0xbfbfebbf "/usr/ports/mail/mailman/work/stage", dest=0x8360bd1 "/") at pkg.c:1207
> #2  0x08055e4d in exec_register (argc=7, argv=0xbfbfea50) at /usr/ports/ports-mgmt/pkg/work/pkg-1.1.4/pkg-static/../pkg//register.c:306
> #3  0x080520ad in main (argc=7, argv=0xbfbfea50) at /usr/ports/ports-mgmt/pkg/work/pkg-1.1.4/pkg-static/../pkg//main.c:666

Code near crash site:

> (gdb) l -
> 186			if (pack->pass) {
> 187				struct passwd *pw = getpwnam(uname);
> 188				archive_entry_set_uid(entry, pw->pw_uid);
> 189			}
> 190			archive_entry_set_uname(entry, uname);
> 191		}
> 192	
> 193		if (gname != NULL && gname[0] != '\0') {
> 194			if (pack->pass) {
> 195				struct group *gr = getgrnam(gname);
> (gdb) l
> 196				archive_entry_set_gid(entry, gr->gr_gid);
> 197			}
> 198			archive_entry_set_gname(entry, gname);
> 199		}
> 200	
> 201		if (perm != 0)
> 202			archive_entry_set_perm(entry, perm);
> 203	
> 204		pkg_config_bool(PKG_CONFIG_UNSET_TIMESTAMP, &unset_timestamp);
> 205	

Variables:

> (gdb) fr 0
> #0  0x080bac08 in packing_append_file_attr (pack=0x28405150, filepath=0xbfbfdd0f "/usr/ports/mail/mailman/work/stage/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/", newpath=0xbfbfd90e "//usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/", 
>     uname=0x28870801 "root", gname=0x28870813 "mailman", perm=0) at packing.c:196
> 196				archive_entry_set_gid(entry, gr->gr_gid);
> (gdb) info locals
> gr = (struct group *) 0x0
> fd = 676422080
> map = 0x28404820 "/usr/local/www"
> retcode = 0
> ret = 0
> st = {st_dev = 78, st_ino = 950711, st_mode = 16877, st_nlink = 2, st_uid = 0, st_gid = 0, st_rdev = 3838263, st_atim = {tv_sec = 1382606524, tv_nsec = 0}, st_mtim = {tv_sec = 1382606524, tv_nsec = 0}, st_ctim = {tv_sec = 1382606524, 
>     tv_nsec = 0}, st_size = 512, st_blocks = 8, st_blksize = 32768, st_flags = 0, st_gen = 1770930059, st_lspare = 0, st_birthtim = {tv_sec = 1382606524, tv_nsec = 0}}
> entry = (struct archive_entry *) 0x285161c0
> sparse_entry = (struct archive_entry *) 0xbfbfd798
> unset_timestamp = 40

NOTE that gname is set, only we could not find the associated /etc/group
record, and dereferencing the 0 gr fails.
